Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows significant renal impairment (markedly elevated creatinine, proteinuria, microalbuminuria), metabolic acidosis (low CO₂, high chloride), and nephrotic-range proteinuria. Urinalysis reveals glucosuria, ketonuria, and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting possible diabetic nephropathy or another glomerular pathology, though HbA1c is unexpectedly low. Dyslipidemia is present (high total cholesterol, LDL, HDL). Immediate nephrology evaluation is recommended for further assessment and management.
